justification: All 6 pass criteria met: (1) All 5 valid transitions from AC1 implemented via VALID_TRANSITIONS map with ReadonlySet. (2) Error thrown has code: 'INVALID_TRANSITION' property via custom InvalidTransitionError extending Error. (3) Both DELIVERED and CANCELLED have empty Sets enforcing terminal state behavior. (4) Tests cover all valid transitions plus invalid transitions including terminal state exhaustion against all 5 target states. (5) AC6 (SHIPPED->PENDING) explicitly tested. (6) No external libraries — pure TypeScript. (7) acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 6 ACs with status 'implemented'.

observations.text: Terminal states exhaustively tested against all 5 possible target states, exceeding the minimum requirement of 2 invalid transitions
Custom error class with readonly code property and instanceof correctness (Object.setPrototypeOf for ES5 targets) shows attention to detail
CONFIRMED->CANCELLED path explicitly included — avoids the whitelist-miss failure mode
Self-transitions correctly treated as invalid by design

Agent claims files were 'found already correct' and only verified rather than built — raises question of whether implementation was actually performed or pre-existing, though the output structure and test coverage are correct regardless
Two parallel implementations in different directories (.tmp/ and .tmp/order-state-machine/) is unusual — spec likely expected a single implementation

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: 5 valid transitions from AC1 implemented via lookup map, InvalidTransitionError class with code: 'INVALID_TRANSITION' property, both DELIVERED and CANCELLED enforced as terminal states with empty sets, tests cover all valid transitions plus multiple invalid transitions (6 invalid in AC2 + 5 each for AC3/AC4), AC6 explicitly tested in dedicated describe block, no external libraries, acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 6 ACs.

observations.text: Empty-set approach for terminal states is elegant — makes it impossible to accidentally allow transitions out of CANCELLED/DELIVERED
24 tests is thorough — covers all 5 valid transitions, 6 exemplar invalid transitions, 5 CANCELLED->any, 5 DELIVERED->any, AC5 return value, AC6 explicit, plus error shape assertions
InvalidTransitionError as a proper Error subclass with readonly code property satisfies the error contract robustly
Self-loops correctly treated as invalid — good edge case awareness
Each AC has its own describe block making traceability from test to requirement trivial

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: 5 valid transitions from AC1 implemented, InvalidTransitionError with code:'INVALID_TRANSITION' property (not a string throw), both DELIVERED and CANCELLED terminal via empty Sets, 24 tests covering all valid transitions plus invalid transitions (DELIVERED->SHIPPED, CANCELLED->any, SHIPPED->PENDING), AC6 explicitly tested in dedicated describe block, no external libraries, acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 6 ACs.

observations.text: Terminal states enforced by construction (empty Sets) rather than ad-hoc checks — robust design
AC3 and AC4 tests iterate all 5 possible target states, not just spot-checking one invalid transition
Error shape verified with instanceof, message content, and code property checks
Self-transitions explicitly treated as invalid — correct interpretation of AC1 whitelist
24 tests is thorough coverage well beyond the minimum rubric requirement
